author: @gifirst1

text: what was your process for making the medley? Like, after you decided on songs, what did you actually do in FL? I'm working on a medley, and was wondering how someone who's done it before multiple times would go around doing it.
author: @ieatatsonic

text:
ieatatsonic Actually, I was deciding songs and working on FL at the same time. I can never figure out a proper progression with my musical sense alone, I need to arrange every song to some degree before I can make any inclusion decisions. Although, while this ensures that you would have every planned song arranged and ready to be mashed up, you could easily end up discarding quite a majority of these songs.  =/
But if you mean what my criteria for choosing these songs were, that may take some more time to explain, and I want to make sure whether or not that is what you're asking before going further with that.
author: @FinnOtaku

text: I don't necessarily mean the criteria, I mean more like the order you did everything in. I'm mainly having trouble deciding whether I should make an entire song's part before I move to the next, or do like the melody first for each part and then go back and do something else, or what.
author: @ieatatsonic

text: ieatatsonic Well, if you want something for reference, other than the original song, you should just transcribe the melody and the bass, preferably the chords as well. No need to do it for the whole song, just the part you're planning to use. And once you have that and couple of more simplified arrangements ready, just try different combinations of them, and see which ones sound good when put together. It's all about intuition, really.
author: @FinnOtaku

author: @NebulonRanger

text:
Nebulon Ranger ...Oh.
Weird... For once, I think my version is actually better than a remix.  O_o
Or at least, this is most likely a result of you just switching around some instruments and not listening the whole thing before uploading:
- Bass for Gamecube Intro has two notes playing at once, and the other has disappeared altogether.
- The synth at the end of Rubicon is supposed to quite down as it transitions to Rockface Rumble.
- The new piano instrument has too sharp sound for the "calm" part, I had to turn down my volume.
- The new strings instrument echoes too much for the project's string notes.
- ...This might be more of a personal preference, but I really like the OBligatory Jump FG synth more than the synth you used to replace it.
That's what I felt was wrong based on one listening. I hope this bit of critique would be of any use.
author: @FinnOtaku

text:
Nebulon Ranger I'll be waiting for the second version then, do what you feel is right with it.  =)
And yeah, C-Side is quite loud compared to this.  =/
author: @FinnOtaku

text: Nebulon Ranger Oh, by the way, you do realize that I added download links to the project's freeware VSTs in the project's readme file, right?
author: @FinnOtaku

text: Yeah, I don't have P8 on this machine so I subbed in one of its siblings. I didn't save over the original FLP though so I should just be able to download them and reopen the original.
author: @NebulonRanger

text:
I updated the MP3 linked in the original post;

Celeste: replaced with a piano from TruePianos' Diamond sample set, which I then EQ'd to remove most of the plonk and bring out the high end, as should be done with trance keys!

Triangle: fixed polyphony, it's triple notes in the gamecube intro, not doubles!

Strings: Bypassed delay, reduced volume a tad.

VRC6N001 arps: switched from SID-6581 to RP2A03 like the rest of the chiptune elements in the medley, finished arp section.

Levels: tweaked a bit to make the resonant saws less harsh-sounding (but still retain their resonance!)

Just click the original link again for the new version!
author: @NebulonRanger

text:
FinnOtaku
Also, in case you're wondering how I fixed the loudness issue C-Side had for this; since I'm using FL on my desktop now as opposed to my laptop, the CPU can handle FG-X a lot more smoothly than the i3 in my laptop. FG-X is a great mastering processor, but man is it demanding on CPU resources.
author: @NebulonRanger

text: Nebulon Ranger ...I have no idea what EQ and resonance means when it comes to music, but at least now this remix sounds way better than before. Now that I somewhat know what to do to make music sound a bit more clearer, I guess my next step would be to learn about equalizing and those kind of things.
author: @FinnOtaku

text:
FinnOtaku EQ is just shorthand for an equaliser pass. Resonance is a term largely applicable to synths that roughly equates to how bright the sound is.

EQ is annoying to get right as you have to set the instrument you want to EQ off against everything else by leaving every track on.
author: @NebulonRanger

text: D-Side remake incoming, and without the stupid low-end CPU artifacting that C-Side had because FL's installed on my six-core i7 desktop now!
